title: get_head_to_head season filter is inconsistent — totals ignore season
evidence: server.py:286-287 filters only the displayed recent matches by season, but data_loader.py:340 compute_head_to_head has no season param, so the reported team1/team2 wins and draws are always all-time even when season is supplied
suggestion: Thread season through compute_head_to_head (via search_matches) so the W/D/L totals and the listed matches use the same scope

title: ATDD prompt asked for unit TDD underneath, but only acceptance tests exist
evidence: prompts/ATDD.md requires 'finer-grained unit TDD underneath to build the internals'; tests/ contains only test_acceptance.py (16 end-to-end tests through mcp.call_tool) — no unit tests for data_loader helpers (normalize_team_name, canonical_competition, compute_standings, etc.)
suggestion: Add unit tests exercising data_loader functions directly to satisfy the ATDD prompt's two-tier expectation

title: BR-Football-Dataset.csv loaded but never queryable
evidence: data_loader.py:109 _load_br_football loads the frame, but _build_unified_matches (data_loader.py:160) merges only brasileirao, copa_do_brasil, libertadores, and historico_only — br_football is excluded, so no tool can reach it; AC-12 (tests/test_acceptance.py:217) 'all six data sources queryable' actually re-queries the unified frames, not br_football
suggestion: Either merge br_football into all_matches or drop the unused loader and correct the AC-12 claim

title: Substring team matching can over-match short queries
evidence: data_loader.py:217-223 uses str.contains(regex=False) on normalized names, so a short team query is a substring of multiple clubs (no word-boundary or exact-name fallback)
suggestion: Prefer exact normalized-name match when available, falling back to substring only when no exact hit

title: Performance tests assert on wall-clock time
evidence: tests/test_acceptance.py:251-267 assert elapsed < 2.0 / < 5.0 seconds, which is environment-sensitive and can flake on slow/loaded CI
suggestion: Gate timing assertions behind a marker or relax/remove them; they verify performance, not the domain behavior
